title: Learning Roadmap Template
description: Backend-agnostic schema for generating course roadmaps like roadmap.sh. v2 — content and progress fully separated.

files.roadmap.json: Pure content definition — cacheable, user-agnostic. This file.
files.user_progress.json: User-specific state — stored separately per user_id + roadmap_id.

key_concepts.topic_graph: All dependency edges live in roadmap.topic_graph[], NOT inside each topic.
key_concepts.variants: Learning path variants let users skip known phases/topics. Defined in roadmap.variants[].
key_concepts.resources: Each topic carries a rich resources[] array with quality, duration, and verification metadata.

roadmap.title: string         — display name e.g. 'Frontend Developer'
roadmap.description: string         — 1–2 sentence summary of what this roadmap covers

phases.name: string  — e.g. 'Foundations'
phases.description: string  — 1 sentence about what this phase covers

topics._comment: No 'status' field here — status lives in user_progress.json only
topics.name: string  — e.g. 'HTML Basics'
topics.description: string  — what the learner will be able to do after completing this

resources.title: string
resources.type: article | video | book | course | docs | practice | podcast
resources.duration_minutes: integer | null  — null for books/articles without a fixed read time

milestone.title: string — e.g. 'Build a static webpage'
milestone.description: string — what the learner produces or demonstrates at phase end

_docs_graph.purpose: Edges are stored here globally, NOT inside each topic, so you can traverse the full graph without loading every topic object.

types.recommended: Completing 'from' before 'to' is strongly advised but not enforced
types.alternative: 'from' and 'to' are interchangeable — learner only needs one

_docs_variants.purpose: Variants let users skip phases or topics they already know, creating a personalised fast-track without duplicating the roadmap definition.
_docs_variants.usage: Present variants at roadmap start as a short quiz or a checklist. Apply the matching variant's skip lists to filter phases[]/topics[] before rendering.

variants.label: string — short display name e.g. 'I already know JavaScript'
variants.description: string — who this variant is for
variants.unlocks_at: string | null — slug of phase/topic where this variant's path rejoins the main roadmap

_separator_label: USER PROGRESS — stored in a separate document / DB table

user_progress.user_id: string | uuid — foreign key to your auth system
user_progress.roadmap_id: string | uuid — foreign key to roadmap.id
user_progress.roadmap_version: string  — semver snapshot this progress record was created against
user_progress.variant_id: string | uuid | null — which variant the user chose, null = default path
user_progress.estimated_completion: ISO 8601 date | null — projected finish date based on pace

topics.topic_id: string | uuid — foreign key to roadmap topic id
topics.note: string | null — learner's personal note on this topic

resource_interactions.resource_id: string | uuid — foreign key to topic resource id

milestones.artifact_url: string | null — link to submitted project, portfolio piece, etc.
